title: Payment Popout UX Refinement
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want clear messaging about the early adopter user offer with a streamlined 'Add More Users' button and Stripe branding, so that I understand the pricing model and trust the payment process.
description:
## Context

The payment popout for purchasing additional users needs refinement to improve messaging and trust signals.

## Changes

### Title Update
Change the title to "Need More Users?" with copy explaining the early adopter offer: 2 free users included, with early adopter pricing for additional users with full feature access (excluding API).

### Button Update
Rename the button to "Add More Users" and open it in a new browser window instead of an inline popout.

### Remove Quantity Input
Remove the quantity input box since Stripe does not support quantity selection on the checkout page.

### Deprecate Request Users
Officially deprecate the old Zapier-based "Request Users" flow.

### Stripe Branding
Add the 'Powered by Stripe' badge below the button, sized similarly to how Stripe displays it on their checkout page — visible but not prominent.
acceptance_criteria: Title displays 'Need More Users?' with early adopter pricing explanation
Button opens Stripe checkout in a new browser window
Old Zapier 'Request Users' flow is deprecated
'Powered by Stripe' badge appears below the button at appropriate size
ux_changes: Redesigned the payment popout with updated title ('Need More Users?'), early adopter messaging, renamed button ('Add More Users'), removed quantity input, added 'Powered by Stripe' badge, and changed the button to open in a new window.
implementation_notes: Stripe's hosted checkout page does not support a quantity selector, so the quantity input was removed from the DataTools Pro UI.
comment_summary: Developer implemented all changes and pushed to staging for review.
